REQUEST FOR COURT JUDGMENT BY DEFAULT, DECLARATION OF NON-MILITARY STATUS, COST BILL $650.00 FILED, CREDIT ACKNOWLEDGED $.00 (TRANSACTION ID # 100069815) AS TO DEFENDANT TOBIAS, ANGEL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ANGEL TOBIAS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT (CCP 473). HEARING IS HELD. THE COURT ANNOUNCES PART OF THE TENTATIVE RULING WAS HEARING REQUIRED; HOWEVER THERE IS NO APPEARANCE BY OPPOSING COUNSEL. THE COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT RULES AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. ALTHOUGH A PROOF OF SERVICE BY A REGISTERED PROCESS SERVER CREATES A PRESUMPTION OF PROPER SERVICE, THE COURT CREDITS MS. TOBIAS' DECLARATION THAT SHE DID NOT RESIDE AT THE SAN FRANCISCO ADDRESS WHERE SUBSTITUTE SERVICE WAS ATTEMPTED BUT WAS LEASING A RESIDENTIAL PROPERTY IN OAKLAND. (TOBIAS DECL. 1; EXH. A.) (SEE ROGERS V. SILVERMAN (1989) 216 CAL.APP.3D 1114, 1124 ["EVEN ACTUAL NOTICE IS NOT A SUBSTITUTE FOR PROPER SERVICE."].) BECAUSE SERVICE WAS NOT EFFECTED AT DEFENDANT'S DWELLING HOUSE OR USUAL PLACE OF ABODE PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 415.20(B), SERVICE WAS IMPROPER AND THE ENTRY OF DEFAULT IS VACATED PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 473(D). (SEE BROWN V. WILLIAMS (2000) 78 CAL.APP.4TH 182, 186 FN.4 ["SECTION 473 EMPOWERS A TRIAL COURT TO SET ASIDE ANY JUDGMENT OR ORDER THAT IS VOID AS A MATTER OF LAW, FOR EXAMPLE, . . . BECAUSE THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT WERE NOT PROPERLY SERVED, SO THAT THE COURT LACKED PERSONAL JURISDICTION OVER A DEFENDANT."].) DEFENDANT SHALL FILE AN ANSWER WITHIN 10 DAYS FROM THE ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: RUTH EKHAUS, CSR#12219, REKHAUS@SFTC.ORG

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-25-2019 Continued To Aug-14-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Supporting Declaration
Off Calendar Jun-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Chambers, Esq. 510-267-7276 of the Law Offices of Carbone, Smith & Koyama for Defendant ANGEL TOBIAS.
No other Appearance

RULING - The matter is Unopposed. Hearing is held. The Court announces part of the tentative ruling was hearing required; however there is no appearance by opposing Counsel. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ANGEL TOBIAS' Motion To Set Aside Default (CCP 473).

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Angel Tobias' motion to set aside default is granted. Although a proof of service by a registered process server creates a presumption of proper service, the Court credits Ms. Tobias' declaration that she did not reside at the San Francisco address where substitute service was attempted but was leasing a residential property in Oakland. (Tobias Decl. 1; Exh. A.) (See Rogers v. Silverman (1989) 216 Cal.App.3d 1114, 1124 ["Even actual notice is not a substitute for proper service."].) Because service was not effected at Defendant's dwelling house or usual place of abode pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 415.20(b), service was improper and the entry of default is vacated pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 473(d). (See Brown v. Williams (2000) 78 Cal.App.4th 182, 186 fn.4 ["Section 473 empowers a trial court to set aside any judgment or order that is void as a matter of law, for example, . . . because the summons and complaint were not properly served, so that the court lacked personal jurisdiction over a defendant."].) Defendant shall file an answer within 10 days from the entry of this order.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Defendant to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Ruth Ekhaus, CSR#12219, rekhaus@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/EPS)
